layer: Miss Lana / booking and lead delivery
description: Replace the demo-only inquiry experience with a reliable production lead pipeline. Implement server-side validation, owner notification to info@misslanatheatre.com, honest success/failure states, spam protection, source attribution, and a minimal durable lead record. Preserve the light form experience and collect only information useful for qualification and response.
non_goals: Do not implement online payment, deposits, customer accounts, availability calendar, automatic quoting, or full CRM automation
Do not publish audience-size price tiers or calculate a final quote
Do not send sensitive private information to analytics
Do not expose secrets, provider keys, private addresses, or internal lead data
Do not redesign unrelated pages or modify domain redirects
Do not promise a customer email copy if the implementation does not send one reliably

decision_basis.origin: User-approved seven-task implementation plan after live-site review and owner questionnaire review on 2026-06-27.
investigation_findings: Current public forms are client-side/demo surfaces and do not provide proven production delivery
Primary destination is info@misslanatheatre.com and Svitlana responds herself within 1–2 business days
Preferred channels include SMS, email, and WhatsApp
Useful qualification fields include event type, date, city/ZIP, venue type, indoor/outdoor, audience size, ages, selected show, and optional add-ons
The user wants the public site light; the form must not become a long procurement questionnaire
Pricing remains quote-based with only From $350 public

must_not_violate: All environment access must go through lib/env.ts
No secrets may be committed or printed
The success state may render only after the server confirms acceptance
Primary owner notification must target info@misslanatheatre.com
The customer must be told Miss Lana will reply within 1–2 business days
Form fields and consent language must not imply a binding booking or final price
Do not collect unnecessary sensitive child data
Public price stays From $350; no quote calculator

forbidden_execution_patterns: No fake success response when delivery or persistence fails
No direct process.env access outside lib/env.ts
No provider-specific credentials in code, fixtures, screenshots, reports, or logs
No logging full free-text message bodies or personal data to public analytics
No broad analytics implementation beyond lead-related events required by this task
execution_guardrails: Choose the smallest production-suitable provider/integration supported by the repository and deployment environment; document the choice
Validate and normalize on the server even if client validation exists
Use rate limiting or equivalent abuse protection plus a honeypot or provider-supported bot control
Keep the default form concise and use conditional fields only where they improve qualification
Store the minimum lead record needed for recovery and attribution
Make delivery failures observable to the owner/operator without exposing private data to users
If provider or account access is unavailable, implement a fully testable adapter and block closure until a live delivery test is completed

readiness_notes: Production email/provider credentials must be available through deployment secrets before live verification

in_scope: LeadForm UX refinement and conditional fields
Optional short customer acknowledgement only if reliably implemented
Minimal durable lead logging/storage with timestamp, source, service, and UTM
Success, validation, network, provider, and retry states
Lead conversion events without personal data
Production delivery tests and operational documentation

steps.title: Define and document the form contract
actions: Finalize required, optional, and conditional fields
Define payload schema, normalization, consent/privacy microcopy, success/failure messages, and attribution fields
Keep the visible form concise and avoid collecting child names except an optional event note if truly necessary
steps.expectation: The client/server contract is explicit and privacy-minimal

steps.title: Implement server-side submission handling
actions: Create the server handler using the repository's supported Next.js pattern
Validate, sanitize, rate-limit, and reject malformed or automated submissions
Generate an internal inquiry ID and normalized event summary
steps.expectation: Invalid or abusive submissions cannot trigger a false success or uncontrolled notification

steps.title: Implement notification and durable recovery
actions: Send the owner notification to info@misslanatheatre.com with all useful event details and source attribution
Persist a minimal lead record in the approved store so provider delivery failure does not silently lose the inquiry
Implement an optional concise customer acknowledgement only if sender configuration and deliverability are proven
steps.expectation: A valid lead is recoverable and the owner receives an actionable notification

steps.title: Implement truthful client states
actions: Connect LeadForm to the server handler
Show field-level validation, submitting, success, recoverable failure, and retry states
Remove demo-form notices only after live pipeline verification
Use the approved success copy and response window
steps.expectation: The user sees success only after acceptance and can recover from failure

steps.title: Add conversion events safely
actions: Emit lead-success, lead-error, phone-click, SMS-click, WhatsApp-click, and email-click events through the existing analytics abstraction or a minimal no-op-safe layer
Exclude names, emails, phone numbers, addresses, comments, and child information from event payloads
steps.expectation: Lead behavior is measurable without leaking personal data

steps.title: Test live delivery and failure paths
actions: Add unit/contract coverage if the repository supports it and e2e coverage for visible form behavior
Perform live production submissions from mobile and desktop
Verify owner delivery, persisted record, optional customer acknowledgement, spam control, and provider-failure handling
steps.expectation: The pipeline is proven end to end, not merely mocked

success_criteria: A valid production inquiry reaches info@misslanatheatre.com and creates a recoverable lead record
The form never displays success for failed validation, failed acceptance, or provider failure
Spam protection and server-side validation are active
Demo-form public notices are removed only after successful live verification
No secrets or personal lead data are exposed in code, logs, reports, or analytics
The visible form remains concise and mobile-usable
Public pricing remains From $350 with no calculator or tier logic
Required commands and live delivery evidence pass

manual_checks: Submit valid inquiries from representative mobile and desktop browsers and verify owner delivery
Force provider/network failure and verify no false success is shown
Verify stored lead fields are minimal and access-controlled
Inspect analytics payloads and logs for personal data leakage
Verify all contact-method links and 1–2 business day success wording
Verify form completion remains reasonable on mobile

closure_sequence: Step 1: Scope verification — confirm all steps, success_criteria, and deliverables are complete; confirm execution-safety compliance (no non_goals fixed, no forbidden_execution_patterns violated, execution_guardrails respected)
Step 2: Automated verification — run only the required_commands explicitly listed for the scoped task, confirm all pass
Step 3: Reviewer-grade pre-closure code review — analyze all changes against the review dimensions, record explicit findings with BLOCKER / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NOTE severity
Step 4: Closure resolution — resolve or explicitly defer findings with severity, write the final verification summary, and update the task report
Step 5: PROJECT_PROGRESS update — append the substantive change entry only after all previous closure steps pass

post_actions: Do not start paid traffic until live delivery, persistence, and failure handling are proven
Record provider/account ownership and recovery steps in the runbook without secrets
